timeline_html:
Summary. You’ve been noticing shedding that comes and goes for over a year, suggesting a pattern that’s settled into waves rather than an acute episode. Your answers point to high daily stress and periods of restrictive eating as steady background factors.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started date not provided)
  • Restrictive dieting / rapid weight loss tendencies (ongoing; date not provided)

Timeline forecast. Because these are ongoing lifestyle and stress-related drivers rather than one-time events, your shedding likely reached its worst several months ago and has been stabilizing in recent months. With steady nutrition and stress recovery, visible thickening usually starts a few months after stability returns. Dominant driver: chronic stress—its long duration and overlap with restrictive eating make it the main ongoing influence, though its improvement can gradually restore balance.

Where you are now. You appear to be in the early regrowth phase—shedding has become more stable, and the baby hairs you’ve seen months ago confirm recovery. Keep supporting your system; consistency is what allows those new hairs to strengthen and fill in. 🌱
